Hi.
Hi, Jessica.
Good afternoon.
How are you?
I am good.
How are you?
Good, thank you.
How is your day going?
Oh,
it's going good.
It goes by fast every day.
So
busy.
Now.
Where in Pennsylvania are you?
So, I'm in central Pennsylvania.
So our home office is in Camp Hill, which is right outside of Harrisburg.
Ok.
So Justin is actually originally, he's from Pennsylvania.
That's why I ask.
Oh, ok.
Where
I, I grew up in, like, Altoona Johnstown area.
Oh, ok.
Yeah, that's like two hours, two hours.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Spend a lot of time in your area though.
I have a lot of friends in, in Philly and I was always making the, the track across the state.
Ok.
All right.
So, are, are you a Philadelphia Eagles fan?
Absolutely not.
No, no.
Ok.
Yeah.
Steeler fan.
I, uh, yeah, we were about 70 miles east of Pittsburgh and then I went to school in Pittsburgh.
So, ok, my entire life I, I'm a Penn State fan though.
I don't know if, if you root for Penn State or if you're more of a kind of temple or
I'm not really caught in too much into college football, to be honest with you.
But yeah,
yeah.
Well, we appreciate you hopping on.
Um, do you have just making sure, do you have a hard stop at 130?
No, I'm ok to go a couple of minutes.
I'm sure, I'm sure that we'll be fine.
Um, I just want to make sure just to be respectful of your time.
Um, so I just want to talk about the agenda.
Um What I had in mind is we would just learn about your current process, just understanding your overall business goals to determine how apartment IQ could fit into your um into that strategy.
Um Yes, and we'll go through the platform and we can showcase what that might look like integrated into your current process.
And then just by the end, we can plan on deciding whether it makes sense for us to continue our conversation or if this is something that you'd like to revisit maybe at a later time down the road.
So that OK, perfect.
Um So to start, I'm just curious in regards to gathering competitive data, market surveys and pricing.
What is that process look like for you guys?
Um If you could just walk us through that.
Sure.

So currently right now, um I'm pretty much using um apartments.com uh costar um and they pull their data from the A Ln, but the challenge I have is not all of our properties advertise on apartments.com.
So the ones that advertise on apartments.com.
Um I can pull a like a market study, market report, but also on that market report, um it normally lists like 1010 comparables.
Um But they're not always comparables.
So my previous background is I actually used to work for costar like eight years ago.
Um and I sold marketing and advertising to apartment communities throughout all of central Pennsylvania.
So I have an idea of different communities, property management companies, that sort of thing.
So some of those comparables that were pulling, um they're, they're just not, it's not apples for apples for what I need for a market study.
So that's where I'm getting some of my information.

The other information is just from um maybe the properties website um calling them, which can be challenging because they always, they don't always wanna give um a competitor, you know, that their information, what their occupancy is and specials.
And then also um some of it I can do through chat bots depending on the way the the property is set up.
Um But that doesn't always, you know, I know that's just by kind of keywords and uh you know, it's not a live person.
So, um but I can get some information that way.
So I'm basically gathering all the information I can.
So at this point, um the market studies that I've done have either been for one of our communities that has an occupancy issue or if we're going to be on boarding a, um, a new property.
So we're, we're a third party management company.
So we manage um, communities for an owner.

Um, so if they're gonna look at us to manage one of their properties, they're probably looking at several other management companies as well and they'd like, um, pricing recommendations um for their amenities and where they are.
So, um, that's the other reason that I would be pulling a market study is if we're gonna be inquiring a new community.
Um So we can see where they are um compared to their competitors too, for us to way and see if it's a good decision for us to make as well if we can help them overcome their challenges.
So I don't have um I don't have a ton of market studies done yet, but when I do them, it's basically an Excel spreadsheet.
Um we use a um like kind of like a template one.
And then it walks through, um I typically try to pull five or six comps per the community and per floor plan.
Um And then some of the communities that we manage that are larger of ours that have 12 floor plans, then that can be a little bit challenging as well.
I'm not gonna do all 12, but if they've got 62 bedroom floor plans, maybe I'll take two of those floor plans and weigh it towards the competitor.
Um But so I'm just looking for, um, year built.
How many units, um, what's their current occupancy?
Um, what's their current uh, rental rate to square foot ratio?
Um, are they running any concessions or specials?
And then it goes through a whole list of amenities.
So, depending on the community, I might, if it's a really high end community, I'm gonna have to add some things and then some things are gonna be obsolete that we wouldn't use.
But then I go through and put the deductions to weight that um price per square footage of the competitors against our property.
Um So it can be extremely time consuming.
It sounds
so time consuming.
Yeah, it's not my favorite part of my job.
Um But what we're, what we're looking to do.
So we have a new um senior VP here and I'm newer to PM I, I just started in October.
Um but we're looking to have a market study for our whole portfolio um updated every quarter.
So for me like looking at building all that out, that's a lot of time.
I mean, understanding updating every quarter would not be, you know, quite as time consuming because the amenities are built in and all of that's built in.
But um so it would just be getting their current rental rates, um specials occupancy, that sort of thing.

Um So the reason what prompted my call to have this call with you is I'm just looking at options to see what's available, see what's out there that I could use as a tool that's gonna have accurate info up to date information because I know the A Ln data that's not always, I've heard from other colleagues in the industry that's not always accurate either.
So.
Right.
Yeah.
Um So thanks for sharing all that.
It sounds like we could probably help with everything that you're looking for.
Um portfolio wise, I was looking at your portfolio um in regards to your conventional units, how many conventional units do you guys have?
I don't know off the top of my head.
II, I would say probably somewhere between 44 and 5000.
Ok.
Yeah, so we have a couple.
So I'm the, I'm the leasing director, but we have them divided up into regions.
So we have different regionals that handle different regions, but I'm the only one that's kind of
doing these tasks if that makes sense.
So we would probably kind of divide them up into those regions and then also the type of property if they're in a class, a property or uh B property, that sort of
thing.
Yeah, for sure.
Um So Justin will get into the platform.
Um But you'll see, we're essentially, we're able to track any competitor as long as they have a website and use a property management software and have online leasing.
Um And we update our data every single day.
So in terms of when you guys go to use the data, you can feel confident that you're, you're making decisions based on updated data from the day prior.
Um just and based on everything she shared.
Did you have any other additional questions before getting into the platform?
Not really, I mean, I think Jamie just kind of touched on it but I mean, in the markets right now, especially with concessions and everything.
I, I think the biggest advantage we're gonna be able to give to you is, is knowing the market.
So, I mean, even with kind of the new process in place where you'd like to ideally have them updated quarterly, I think you're missing out on, on quite a bit there.

Um, I was gonna ask in terms of like setting your rates, is that kind of done on a quarter by quarter basis or is that like if occupancy low you're gonna revisit things or how do you, how do you go about with setting setting rates?
Yeah.
So we typically only adjust our rental rates twice a year.
Um, we don't use like an lro or rent maximizer or anything like that to, um, to adjust our rental rates.
So it's normally the beginning of the year and then the middle of the year.
Um, something we would be using it for, I guess quarterly would be if we need to make a concession or offer a special to have that edge over our competitor.
Makes
sense.
Cool.

Well, yeah, I mean, the, the nice thing here is, is everything centralized and if something comes up where you do get a new client, I mean, you can just jump into our platform and you can see exactly what's going on today.
But the way this is set up is that each one of your properties gets its own individual property card.
And then we run the analytics based off of that property specific concept.
Um I know, excuse me, with co star, you said they kind of give you some some comps that aren't necessarily relevant.
Uh What's great about us is that you're in complete control of your comp set.
So one if you know your market really well and you say, hey, this is the list of properties we wanna track, you can give them to us uh to us so we can set it up that way.
However, we also have a comp matching algorithm where basically what we do is we look at every property in the vicinity of your property, we assign a score to it and we make recommendations.

You know, we say, hey, we think maybe we should track this property or maybe, you know, we don't think you should track this property so we can give some recommendations into who, who we think you should track.
But nonetheless, you're in complete control and you always have the ability to actually add or remove a property to your comp set directly inside the app.
Here itself as well.
Ok.
Ok.
Um One thing I mentioned too is we offer unlimited users.
So I know with co star they charge per seat, it can get very expensive.
Um We offer unlimited users so a lot of our customers will give access to their on site teams or regionals.
So if that's something you have interest in, in, uh you know, we can definitely set them up as well.
Ok.
Ok.
But yeah, to, to view the analytics for a property, you click the view button and it'll take you inside the data.
Uh Once you're inside of here, the very top, this is your main navigation.
So each property is four reports associated with it.
Uh The first report which we're on here is the market survey.
And then if you still have the data drop down, there's gonna be three additional reports as well.
So I'll go with one very high level.
Um But before I do, I want you to know that all of the data inside of apartment IQ is filterable by these different options.
So you can look at based on a specific comp or even group of coms.
Uh You can look at a base off of bedrooms, bathrooms, square footage range, rent, price range, et cetera.
Uh Down below here, all of these metrics are sort from high to low and low to high.
And lastly, it's nice, we actually give each user the ability to customize and toggle on and off whatever metrics are most important to them.
So this is nice.
But yeah, I mean, look at the market survey here, I think very simple, very straightforward, very representative of your standard market survey.
Uh We simply list all of your competitors across the top.
And then we also include your property, the subject property here in the indigo that we can easily make comparisons.
And then all of the data is accessible via vertical scrolling.
So two different sections.
The first one being property summary, that's all that high level property data.
So proximity to how far away is each competitor from your property?
You built total number of units, the occupancy percentage, we also show the exposure percentage.
So the percent of units that are available in each property um as you can see, we provide three different time horizons there.
So total exposure exposure over the next 30 the next 60 days, then we'll show you the number of units that each property has available um of those units that are available.
How many are vacant down below here?
Again, what is really nice is we will let you know if there is a rent special or concession tied to the property or tied to a specific unit in the property.

Um Also you'll see that we do take a step further and we will calculate that effective rent and that effective price per foot for you at that property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the actual unit level.
And lastly, what's nice is we'll actually give you some uh some transactional data as well.
So we'll show you the number of leases each property has had over the last 30 days, the last seven days and the number of leases that each property averages per week.

So, yeah, so it's one of those things, I mean, if you're in a market, maybe, you know, one of your properties is struggling with the occupancy, you can look at the transactional data, you can sort it from high to low.
And you can understand, you know, hey, why are these properties having more success leasing units than us?
Based off of this example, here, we can look down the list and every other property out there is offering a concession except for our property.
It's almost like the perceived value of a concession.
It seems like from a, from a renter's perspective, you know, it feels like they're getting something from it.
So seems like offer.
I mean, in this, in this market, in particular, it seems like probably offering a concession is important.
Um But what's nice too is you'll notice at the top that with these badges, we do rank everything.
So no matter which metric you sort like if you want to see which property has the most amount of units available, you can see that we'll tag resident a Wakefield is having the most available.
Our property, we actually six out of eight.
So we have the the six most units available.
Ok.
Um One thing it's nice too is at the top.
If you actually click in any of these images, we'll pull the property for you.
We'll tell you more information about that competitor.
So for the residents of Wakefield, you know, we'll show you the property address who currently manages them.
We give you a link to the property website, a link to the Google Maps.
And then down below here, we'll actually show you every single unit that's currently being advertised as available in the property right now.
Ok.
The automation here, I mean, just clicking a couple buttons is a lot better than you having to go out there and kind of collect l yourself manually, right?
Um But yeah, I mean, everything we're showing you here, just just so you're aware too is coming directly from a property website.
So this is all the information that a renter would see whenever he or she would visit that site, whether it be yours or or a competitor.
Ok.
Um Second section down here, I think is probably the most beneficial for you or most valuable.
So, pricing and availability.
So this is broken up by floor plan.
So for our example, here, we're looking at one bedrooms, two bedrooms, three bedrooms.
Um Obviously, if your property, your concept of studios, four bedrooms, five bedrooms, those would be included as well.
But the idea here is to essentially understand, you know, exactly where do our one bedrooms fit in comparison to the competition.
And inside of here, I think we give you every key metric to help understand that.

So gross rent again, we'll let you know if there's a concession attached to this specific floor plan, we'll show you net effective rent, square footage price per foot, net effective price per foot and then the number of one bedrooms each property has available.
So what would you like to base this off of, you know, net effective rent or net effective price per foot?
You can sort this from high to low.
You can see who has the most expensive one bedrooms, who is the least expensive one bedrooms and then ultimately, where our one bedrooms are positioned.
So again, is this in line with our strategy or are we worried about occupancy?
Um are we looking to push rent rates?
So it's kind of, you know, putting some of the pieces together in terms of, of where we're at and what the competition is doing as well?
Ok.
Ok.
And, and I think what's nice too is even looking at the concessions.
Um having a little bit of context here is nice.
I mean, are they offering to waive app or admin fees or are they offering, you know, a month free?
So obviously, if, if, if it's time to, you know, if you look out in the markets, you know, offering concessions and it kind of triggers your property to, to, to offer them as well.
Um It's important to kind of understand the depth of the context of the concessions being offered.
And again, this is a very easy way to, to understand that.
Ok.
Um Like I said, a bunch of other reports here, I'll just go over them high level.
Um This unit type report, this is every, every floor plan that's currently available uh in your comp set.
So at the top you get a nice little um illustration that breaks out the average price per floor plan.

So um what's nice is in here is we also show you the percent change of each floor plan over the last seven days and then down below that, we'll give you a line graph that illustrates the trends of each floor plan over the last uh 12 weeks as well.

Um So very macro view of your entire comp set again, every property in your comp set, but again, utilizing these filters, what's nice is you can look at specific subsets of comps and maybe you know, these three properties are who you feel like you truly compete against, who who you're most likely to lose a rent or two.
So clicking that populate the data, so you can see how just those three properties are pricing their their floor plans.
OK.
Yeah, I think uh average days on market is nice as Well, so we show that at a floor plan level.
So again, sorting this from low to high and understanding why some of these properties are getting their one bedrooms off the market quicker than us.

Again, for this example, I mean, it looks like uh the village apartments, you know, they're, they're offering a free month's rent and, and waiving app and admin fees, whereas our property we're not doing anything.
Um You know, so maybe that is a little bit to do with it.
So again, it's like I said earlier, putting the pieces of the puzzle together.
OK?
One thing I wanted to show you here, um which I think is really valuable.
So even like I said earlier, if you, if you guys are collecting data once a quarter, um you know, the data is essentially worthless the very next day.
You know, these properties out there like we talked about are using LRO or they're using yields star, they're changing rates regularly.
So I do like to show this to emphasize the importance of daily data, but our system actually keeps track of every single time a unit makes a rate change.
This particular metric shows you the number of uni or sorry, the number of rent changes for each unit over the last 30 days.
So I sort it from high to low.
But you can see right here that, you know, this unit's made 22 rate changes over the last 30 days 20 rate changes, 19 rate changes and so on.
So again, it's one of the things that, you know, the competition is changing rates, you know, regularly, almost daily.
So to truly understand your market, I think you do need, you know, quality data.

So again, a big advantage with us is, is even if you are only changing rates, you know, uh, once a quarter or twice a year, I'm just being able to jump in and know you're getting the database off of today rather than, you know, a couple of months ago, right?
Ok.
Um One thing that I think is pretty cool with this too is I can actually help with pricing renewals.
Um I oftentimes hear that renewals are based off of historical pricing or they're based off of like today, you know, March 19th numbers.
Uh The example I use is let's pretend you have a couple of two bedrooms are up for renewal in May.
Um What you can actually do here is you can look and see how the competition is pricing their bedrooms that are available in May as well.
So kind of doing like an apples to apples pricing comparison.
So what you would do here is you would actually toggle on two bedrooms and then you go over the date available, you come all the way over to May and highlight May.
Then all the data down here is gonna populate based on two bedrooms that are available in May.
So you can see that there's currently 24 available.
The average price per foot of those is a dollar 47 average rent price is 1495.
And then if you want to get really granular, you can see the unit level data for all 24 of those two bedrooms that are available in May.
So it's one of those things that, you know, obviously it's, it's a lot more cost efficient to, to retain a renter than go find a new one.
So you don't want to necessarily over price your their renewal.
So they're kind of forced to look somewhere else.
But at the same time, you don't wanna underprice it and, and leave some meat on the bone.
So this is just again, just a nice way to look out there and see how the competition is pricing their, their units that are available, you know, during the same time frame.
Ok.
Yep.
And then I know you said that you guys deal uh with a lot of third party management, a lot of ownership groups, clients.
Um We do have some reporting that's available.
So these reports are downloadable as frequently an off as you'd like.
You can also automate them to be delivered to an inbox on whatever cadence you'd like as well.
So, weekly, monthly, quarterly, um but this is what they look like.
They're property specific.
Um We time stamp them down here in the bottom left that way whoever is looking at the data is on the same page as far as whenever it's been pulled.

But inside of here you get high level market summary information, you get high level property insights and what's nice too is you'll, you'll see that we do show you the percent change of each metric over the last seven days.
So your eyes are really drawn to any anomalies or anything that does stick out.
Um And then we also do a unit type analysis for every floor plan that's available in your comp set.
So these are the averages based off of every one bedroom out there.
And then below that, we give you the database off of each property that does have one bedrooms available.
And lastly, we provide some concession reporting.
So how many of the properties in your comp set are offering concessions?
What's the average value of those concessions?
And then down below here, we'll show you the details for each of those concessions that the property is offering.
Ok.
Ok.
But that's essentially apartment IQ like I said, we can automate everything.
I know we can definitely give you better data.
Um You touched on a Ln just so you, you, I don't know if you know how A Ln gets their data, but they have call farms set up and, and they promise to touch each account once a month.
So they'll call a property that property doesn't answer this month.
Um That still counts as their touch for the month.
So it's not updated or try to be updated again until the following month.
Yeah.
Yeah, I get a lot of this phone call.
We, yeah, we,
we manage, um, quite a few properties internally here from the home office that aren't large enough to have a prop, like an office on site.
So we get all the phone calls for those properties.
Yeah,
I think for a long time it was, I mean, really until COVID, whenever online leasing was extremely accelerated, that was the only way to get this information.
So I think it was the best solution at the time, but things have changed.
So.
Yeah.
OK.
Um Well, II I definitely like um the product and the analytics.
Um I have a couple questions.
Um So I guess my first question will be um and this is asking because some of our communities are set up um that they, that we don't have live availability right now.
So we were working off a wait list and I'm transitioning um those communities to all be live online and then the ones that advertise on an Ils um for that to also pull over to the ILS.
Um But when your systems pulling the data and the data, um I'm assuming it can only pull from communities that have a website with live availability.
That's correct.
Yeah.
Yeah.

So you guys use Yardi right around Cafe.

Our system can actually go in and get that that information from you already.

If it says one available, we can get unit level information.
Ok?
Very good.

So most of the time they're always gonna be on a wait list.

got like the online lease.

and scroll down.

So as soon as we would put someone on notice, then they would appear um, online.
But I, I had a, a question for you on that and this is just kind of like a census question, I guess.
Um, with you working with other property management companies.
So do you find that most property management companies display their up upcoming availability, 60 days out or more than 60 days out?
So it seems like a lot of systems constrict you or restrain you whatever word I'm looking for there to, to 60 days.
Like I know in Trotta we will only list out 60 days.
I have some customers and, and even like in my demo account, I think I can show you availability up there like July.
So it literally just depends on it's more so I think they're um the P MS that they work with rather than like their preference.
Ok, because we, so with rent cafe, um it is just a setting issue that I can adjust.
Um And, and right now we have it set to 60 days um but we could put it to zero and it if we typically only take notice 75 days prior to move out.
So, but if you had a fluke, that was like three months from, we could set it that it would show live online.
But our concern, I guess is um screening individuals like three or four months in advance before they're moving in.
Um And I was just curious because I, I feel like most communities require a 60 day notice nowadays.
Um but for the renter, they give their 60 day notice and then they've got 60 days to find somewhere new because they're not seeing availability online pretty much prior to that.
So I was just curious.
It's just a common thing I hear here.
People put tickets into me and ask me um what you have found like the overall census with the way the market is right now and with people advertising their availability.

Yeah, I think, um like I said, I think, and try to limit you, I don't think they give you the option, but I think everybody else, like what I'm showing you right here is, is whenever proper start to make new units available.
So like for here, there's 22 bedrooms that are available all the way out in July.
I mean, June would be, you know, farther than 60 days.
Um So a lot of, a lot of May would be too.
So one of those things, I mean, what's, what's i, it just depends on the strategy.
Like there's some customers or some property management companies that, you know, this, these units out in July, they might list them, but they're gonna put a higher rent price with them.
You know, they say, hey, they're not necessarily have to worry about them for a month or two or three months.
So let's try to achieve a way higher rent price.
It's honestly just kind of strategy, whatever, whatever makes sense.
I, I think to your point, like you said, if you had to put in notice for 60 days, like you gotta start looking for a place too within, you know, 6070 days.
So I think, I think having it there and creating a better renter experience is kind of what it's about, but it's, it's up to every, it's, you know.
Ok.
Yeah, I just, I just wondered, I mean, like I, I tried to update ours to like 75 days but it doesn't let you do that.
So it goes by actual months.
So it's either with rent cafe, so it's either two months or three months.

that's why we give you the uh the exposure percentage too over over the different time horizons because I mean, obviously you can look at it as a whole, but you can look over the next 30 days like let me like over the next 30 days.

Like is that included in their rent or is an additional fee.
